rationale: The cited paper studies response-style bias in Likert-based marketing attitude scales across 11 EU countries, whereas the citing paper develops a hierarchical Bayesian hybrid conjoint model for integrating self-stated importances with profile evaluations and choice prediction. Given this strong topical/method mismatch and the matched evidence being only a bare DOI reference (no contextual snippet indicating methodological use), this looks like an incidental/background citation rather than a substantive dependency.
